title: tense-gate lint: enforce vision.md is present-tense only (rejects will/should/STUB/TBD)
description:
Foundation lint script that scans vision.md (and any document declared as 'prescriptive present') for future-tense markers and blocks them. Implements the 'vision has no future tense' rule mechanically.

**Why P1**: this is the single mechanism that makes /vision-author and /vision-review trustable. Without it, 'vision is present-tense only' is convention. With it, the rule is enforced at every save and every commit. Also immediately useful on existing mira-adapters vision.md (will flag current STUB sections).

## Markers to flag (case-insensitive)

- `will <verb>` (e.g. 'will support', 'will be implemented')
- `should <verb>` (in vision context — 'should be tested' is acceptance criteria, not vision)
- `plan to` / `planning to` / `going to`
- `by Q[1-4]`, `by [month]`, `by [year]`
- `eventually`, `later`, `in the future`
- `TBD` anywhere
- `STUB` anywhere
- `to be locked by`, `to be decided`, `to be determined`
- `TODO` (in vision.md only — not in code comments elsewhere)

## Implementation

- PEP 723 Python script (~80 LOC, regex-based)
- Path: claude-code-plugins/scripts/tense-gate.py (or new dedicated plugin)
- Three integration points:
  1. **CLI**: `tense-gate.py docs/vision.md` — exits 0 clean, 1 on violations
  2. **Pre-commit hook**: provided .pre-commit-hooks.yaml entry, scoped to docs/vision.md and any file with frontmatter `document_type: prescriptive-present`
  3. **Skill integration**: called from /vision-author at write-time and /vision-review before saving edits

## Output format

Per violation:
```
docs/vision.md:42:1: [will] Future-tense marker in prescriptive document.
  Found: 'The platform will support multi-tenant routing'
  Suggest: rewrite as principle ('The platform supports multi-tenant routing') OR move to bead/roadmap
```

## Allowlist

Comments with `<!-- tense-gate-ignore: <reason> -->` on the preceding line skip a single line. Use sparingly; document reason.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Script exists with PEP 723 frontmatter, executable
- [ ] All listed markers detected
- [ ] CLI exits 0 on clean / 1 on violations with actionable output
- [ ] .pre-commit-hooks.yaml entry provided
- [ ] Allowlist mechanism (`tense-gate-ignore` comment) works
- [ ] Smoke test on existing mira-adapters/docs/vision.md: lists current STUB/TBD/'to be locked' sections as violations
- [ ] README/docs explain which markers are flagged and why
acceptance_criteria: Script flags all future-tense markers in vision.md; pre-commit hook integration works; smoke test on mira-adapters vision.md surfaces existing STUB sections as violations
notes: Foundation for /vision-author and /vision-review skills. Sibling beads will depend on this. Born from the 2026-04-19 mira-agent reframe: vision has no future tense; anything future-tense belongs in beads.

title: [CHORE] Bead-Pflicht-Sektion 'Architecture Contracts Touched' + bd lint-Check
description:
Führt eine Pflicht-Sektion in der Bead-Description ein: welche Contracts werden berührt, welche Helper/Enforcer kommen zum Einsatz, welche Gaps sind zu schließen. Erzwungen durch bd lint --check=architecture-contracts.

## Template-Sektion

```markdown
## Architecture Contracts Touched
- ADR-NNN (Name): <was der Bead tut, wie er den Contract nutzt>
- Helper: <pfad/zu/helper.ts>
- Enforcer-Proactive: <codegen/builder>
- Enforcer-Reactive: <lint-rule oder test>

## Coverage Expected
- Packages: <liste der berührten Packages>
- Status nach Bead: <was wird grün in der Matrix>

## Gaps to Close
- [ ] Keine / oder: [ ] [ADR-NEEDED] ..., [HELPER-NEEDED] ..., [ENFORCER-*-NEEDED] ...
```

## Enforcement

bd lint --check=architecture-contracts prüft bei Beads mit Label 'touches-contract', ob die Sektionen vorhanden sind. Fehlt sie → fail.
design: Check läuft als externes Skript, von bd lint geladen. Parser: einfache Regex gegen die drei Section-Header. Output: strukturierte Fehler mit Zeilenangabe im Bead-Markdown.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] beads-workflow/skills/create/SKILL.md dokumentiert neue Pflicht-Sektion
- [ ] beads-workflow/skills/create liefert Template mit den 3 Sub-Sektionen
- [ ] beads-workflow/scripts/bd-lint-contracts.sh (oder .py) als neuer Check
- [ ] bd lint --check=architecture-contracts funktioniert (exit 1 bei Fehlern)
- [ ] Doku in beads-workflow/skills/create/references/contract-sections.md
- [ ] Label 'touches-contract' als Konvention dokumentiert
notes:
Parent: CCP-6up. Abhängig von: Vokabular-Split. Unabhängig von anderen P1-Beads — parallel machbar.
## Refinements migrated from CCP-u87 (Reading Corpus, superseded 2026-04-19)

The 'Architecture Contracts Touched' Pflicht-Sektion is the right home for the Reading-Corpus mechanism. Specific additions:

- **Per-bead-type × per-layer routing** (not flat list). Feature/refactor/bug × layer:* matrix. Flat 'always read these' lists get ignored.
- **Source: .claude/project-config.yml** (per project). Projects without this file get a degenerate single-layer config — no harness surprise.
- **Named/parseable section in project-context template** (locked H2 header + table format) so other tools can consume without re-parsing prose.
- **Surface BOTH vision.md AND project-context.md** in the corpus. vision.md is primary input during bead-creation (prescriptive principles); project-context.md is primary during implementation (descriptive stack).
- **Tense gate (NEW foundation bead)**: vision.md sentences with future-tense markers (will/should/plan to/by Q[n]/eventually/TBD/STUB/to be locked) are rejected. Mechanically enforces the 'vision has no future tense' model. See sibling bead [tense-gate].

Example .claude/project-config.yml shape:
```yaml
reading_corpus:
  bead_creation:
    default: [docs/vision.md, docs/adr/]
    by_layer:
      platform: [docs/vision.md#principles, docs/adr/ADR-005-identity.md]
      ig: [docs/vision.md#13, https://npm.cognovis.de]
  implementation:
    default: [docs/project-context.md]
    by_layer:
      pvs-adapter: [packages/pvs-adapter/CLAUDE.md, docs/architecture/pvs-integration.md]
```
Plan Review Gate: CRITICAL — Three blocking issues: (1) bd lint --check=architecture-contracts is impossible on compiled binary — invocation mechanism unresolved; (2) No mechanism defined to enumerate beads labeled 'touches-contract'; (3) Parser strictness/section validation not specified. Bead reset to open pending decision on these load-bearing questions.

title: [FEATURE] project-context: Enforcement-Matrix-Sektion generieren
description:
Erweitert dev-tools/skills/project-context um eine automatisch generierte Enforcement-Matrix pro Repo. Beantwortet die Frage 'welche 5 Docs muss ich lesen bevor ich X anfasse'.

## Problem

Docs sind heute verstreut (overview, project-context, feature docs, package CLAUDE.md, beads). Kein Single-Index.

## Lösung

Matrix-Tabelle Contract × Package × (ADR / Helper / Proactive / Reactive) als neue Sektion in docs/project-context.md. Leere Zellen = sichtbare Gaps.

Beispiel:
| Contract       | adapter-common | adapter-cli | pvs-x-isynet | pvs-charly | pvs-xdt |
|----------------|----------------|-------------|--------------|------------|---------|
| ID Taxonomy    | ❌ (hoist)    | ❌          | ✅           | ⚠️ teil    | ❌       |
| Schema Codegen | n/a            | n/a         | ✅           | ❌          | ❌       |

## Generierung

- ADRs: Scan docs/adr/**/*.md Frontmatter für 'contract', 'applies_to', 'status'
- Helpers: Scan packages/**/src/ für Helper-Index (per Convention: jsdoc-Tag oder export-Name)
- Proactive: Scan für Codegen-Skripte (scripts/*gen*.ts, package.json 'schema:*')
- Reactive: Scan für Lint-Rules (eslint.config.js Regel-Namen), Test-Scripts (package.json 'check:*')

## Scenario
**Precondition:** A repository checked out locally with at least: (a) `docs/adr/` directory containing one or more `*.md` ADR files with YAML frontmatter exposing `contract:`, `applies_to:` (list of package names), and `status:` (`accepted` | `proposed` | `superseded`); (b) one or more `packages/*/` directories holding source code under `src/` and a `package.json`; (c) optional `eslint.config.js` (or `.eslintrc.*`) at repo root or per-package; (d) optional codegen scripts under `scripts/` and/or `package.json` scripts matching `schema:*`, `*gen*`, `check:*`. The skill `dev-tools/skills/project-context` is installed and the existing `docs/project-context.md` either does not exist yet or is overwritable (`--force` or interactive confirm).
**Action:** The user runs `/project-context` (or `/project-context --force`) from the repository root. The skill executes its existing Phase 0–4 workflow plus the new Enforcement-Matrix scan pass: it globs `docs/adr/**/*.md`, parses YAML frontmatter to derive `(contract, applies_to[], status)` tuples; globs `packages/*/` to derive the column set; scans each package's `src/` for helper exports/jsdoc tags tagged with a contract; scans `scripts/*gen*.{ts,js,py}` and `package.json` `schema:*` / `*gen*` script names for proactive enforcers; scans `eslint.config.js` rule names and `package.json` `check:*` scripts for reactive enforcers. Results are reduced to one cell per `(contract, package)` intersection across four sub-columns (ADR / Helper / Enforcer-Proactive / Enforcer-Reactive).
**Expected Result:** `docs/project-context.md` contains a new top-level section `## Enforcement Matrix` (placed after `Module Map`, before `Established Patterns`) with: (1) a Markdown table whose rows are contracts (sorted alphabetically) and whose columns are packages (sorted alphabetically); (2) each cell shows four status symbols in fixed order `ADR | Helper | Proactive | Reactive` using `✅` (present), `⚠️` (partial — e.g. ADR exists but `status: proposed`, or helper exists but no enforcer), `❌` (missing), `n/a` (declared not-applicable via ADR `applies_to` exclusion); (3) a legend below the table explaining the four sub-columns and four symbols; (4) a "Gaps" sub-section listing every `(contract, package)` pair where any sub-column is `❌`, sorted by gap-count descending; (5) the skill's Phase 4 report includes a `gap-found` signal line `Enforcement gaps: N (across M contracts)` that downstream skills (`/allowlist-burndown`, `wave-orchestrator` Coverage-Gate) can grep for.

### Test Data
Fixture project at `dev-tools/skills/project-context/tests/fixtures/mira-adapters-snapshot/` mirroring a slice of the real mira-adapters monorepo. Layout:
- `docs/adr/` — 3 ADRs:
  - `0001-id-taxonomy.md` (frontmatter: `contract: ID Taxonomy`, `applies_to: [adapter-common, pvs-x-isynet, pvs-charly]`, `status: accepted`)
  - `0002-schema-codegen.md` (`contract: Schema Codegen`, `applies_to: [pvs-x-isynet, pvs-charly, pvs-xdt]`, `status: accepted`)
  - `0003-error-envelope.md` (`contract: Error Envelope`, `applies_to: [adapter-common]`, `status: proposed` — drives `⚠️`)
- `packages/adapter-common/` — `src/id-taxonomy.ts` (helper export, no enforcer); `package.json` with `check:ids` script (reactive enforcer present).
- `packages/adapter-cli/` — `src/index.ts`, `package.json`, no helpers, no enforcers (every cell `❌` or `n/a`).
- `packages/pvs-x-isynet/` — `src/id-taxonomy.ts` + `src/schema/` directory, `scripts/gen-schema.ts` (proactive enforcer), `package.json` with `schema:check` script (reactive), `eslint.config.js` with rule `mira/no-raw-id`. Full `✅` row.
- `packages/pvs-charly/` — `src/id-taxonomy.ts` (helper), missing codegen script (drives `⚠️ teil` for Schema Codegen).
- `packages/pvs-xdt/` — empty `src/`, no enforcers (drives `❌` row to demonstrate gap detection).
- Expected golden output: `tests/fixtures/mira-adapters-snapshot/expected-enforcement-matrix.md` (snapshot of the rendered section the unit test asserts against).

### Variants
1. **Empty matrix**: project with `docs/adr/` empty (or absent) and any package layout — section renders header + legend + "No contracts declared yet — add ADRs to populate this matrix." placeholder, no table, no `gap-found` signal.
2. **No packages**: project with ADRs but no `packages/*/` directories (single-package repo or `src/` at root) — section renders a single-column table where the column is the repo root, contracts still listed as rows.
3. **Partial cell (`⚠️`)**: ADR exists with `status: proposed`, OR helper present but no enforcer of either kind, OR proactive present but reactive missing — cell shows `⚠️` and the Gaps sub-section lists the partial pair.
4. **Full gap row**: contract declared in an ADR but no package has a helper, proactive, or reactive enforcer — entire row renders `❌` across all `applies_to` packages, `gap-found` signal triggers and Phase 4 report flags this contract first in the gaps list.
5. **n/a propagation**: ADR `applies_to` excludes a package — that `(contract, package)` cell shows `n/a` in all four sub-columns and is NOT counted as a gap.
6. **Re-run idempotency**: running `/project-context --section=enforcement-matrix` on a repo whose docs/project-context.md already contains the section regenerates only that section in place, preserves the rest of the file, and emits the same gap count for unchanged inputs.
design: Scanner als Python-Skript (stdlib, kein uv nötig — nur glob+regex). Läuft als Teil des project-context skill. Outputs JSON zwischen Scan-Passes, rendert Markdown-Tabelle am Ende.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] dev-tools/skills/project-context erweitert um enforcement-matrix-Generierung
- [ ] Ausgabe im generierten docs/project-context.md unter neuer Sektion 'Enforcement Matrix'
- [ ] Status-Symbole: ✅ vollständig, ⚠️ teilweise, ❌ fehlt, n/a nicht zutreffend
- [ ] Unit-Test mit mira-adapters-Snapshot als Fixture
- [ ] Empty cells führen zu 'gap-found'-Signalen im skill output
notes:
Parent: CCP-6up. Abhängig von: Vokabular-Split. Blockiert: /allowlist-burndown, wave-orchestrator Coverage-Gate.
## Refinement migrated from CCP-u87 (Reading Corpus, superseded 2026-04-19)

The Enforcement-Matrix-Sektion in project-context should also acknowledge that vision.md is a peer prescriptive document, not just a downstream consumer. Specifics:

- **bead-orchestrator Phase 2 loads BOTH files**: vision.md (prescriptive present, principles + boundaries) + project-context.md (descriptive present, stack + patterns). One-line widening of current Phase 2 logic.
- **Routing**: vision.md primary during /create (boundaries, principles, anti-goals); project-context.md primary during implementation (stack, patterns, established conventions).
- vision.md is the source of truth for the boundary table that architecture-scout (CCP-2hd) parses; project-context Enforcement-Matrix is the source of truth for which Trinity artifacts (ADR/Helper/Enforcer) exist per contract.
- Together they form the constitution: vision = prescriptive ('what MUST hold'), project-context = descriptive ('what IS true').

title: [FEATURE] Agent architecture-scout: Coverage-Matrix-Report vor /plan
description:
Read-only Agent, der VOR /plan und /epic-init läuft und eine Coverage-Matrix (Contract × Package × ADR/Helper/Proactive/Reactive) produziert. Erkennt Missing-ADRs, Hoist-Schulden, Pre-Trinity-Packages.

## Problem

Heute startet /plan ohne expliziten Contract-Check. Implizite Annahmen über IDs, Scoping, Delta-Semantik bleiben unsichtbar bis ein Bug sie freilegt.

## Vorgehen

- Neuer Agent: meta/plugins/architecture-trinity/agents/architecture-scout.md
- Tools: Read, Grep, Glob (read-only)
- Input: Domain-Kontext (Bead-Beschreibung + berührte Pfade)
- Output: Coverage-Matrix mit drei Sektionen:
  1. Vorhandene Contracts (Pfad:Zeile Pflichtlektüre)
  2. Implizite Contracts erkannt (Heuristik: stringly-typed, nur-in-einer-Datei-Rules, wiederkehrende bd search-Treffer)
  3. Empfohlene Tripel mit Stub-Titeln

## Integration

/plan und /epic-init spawnen den Scout am Anfang und hängen Report ans Plan-Dokument. wave-orchestrator nutzt den Report als Gate (separater Bead).
design: Agent lebt in neuem Plugin meta/plugins/architecture-trinity/. Nutzt Grep auf docs/adr/**/*.md + docs/architecture/**/*.md + packages/**/src/**/*.ts für Pattern-Erkennung. Keine eigenen Dateien schreiben — nur Report zurückgeben an Caller.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Agent meta/plugins/architecture-trinity/agents/architecture-scout.md existiert mit Frontmatter (tools: Read, Grep, Glob)
- [ ] Agent produziert Coverage-Matrix-Output in definiertem Markdown-Format
- [ ] Heuristik für Implizite-Contract-Erkennung dokumentiert (mindestens 3 Pattern)
- [ ] /plan skill ruft architecture-scout auf und hängt Output ans Plan-Dokument
- [ ] /epic-init skill ruft architecture-scout pro Subtask auf
- [ ] Test-Fixture: ein Beispiel-Matrix-Report in references/example-matrix.md
notes:
Parent: CCP-6up. Abhängig von: Vokabular-Split (CCP-xxx). Blockiert: wave-orchestrator Coverage-Gate. Sonny/Haiku-fähig.
## Refinements migrated from CCP-k1u (conformance-reviewer, superseded 2026-04-19)

architecture-scout absorbs the conformance-review function. Key additions:

**Two run modes** (configurable in .claude/project-config.yml):

- `advisor` (default): findings surfaced as DECIDE items to user; orchestrator continues
- `gate`: BLOCKING findings stop bead-orchestrator at Phase 0; plan does not proceed

Default advisor so the agent doesn't block forward motion. Projects opt into gate when their vision/invariants are stable.

**Machine-parseable JSON output** (consumed directly by wave-orchestrator and bead-orchestrator):
```json
{
  "status": "CONFORM" | "VIOLATION",
  "mode": "advisor" | "gate",
  "findings": [
    { "rule": "vision §4 boundary: layer:platform must not depend on layer:application",
      "concern": "...",
      "severity": "BLOCKING" | "ADVISORY",
      "source": "docs/vision.md#L142" }
  ]
}
```

**Locked boundary table format in vision.md template.** The boundary table has fixed columns: rule | scope | source-section. Lock the format in the vision.md template so parsing is reliable.

**Drift detection direction is one-way**: code-violates-stated-principle ONLY. The 'vision-behind' case is impossible by construction — vision has no future tense (anything future-tense is a bead, not a principle). This collapses what was originally CCP-93e (drift report) into a simpler check.

**Skip flag**: CONFORMANCE_SKIP=1 env var bypass for emergency unblocks.

title: [CHORE] Trinity-Vokabular: Split enforcer → enforcer-proactive + enforcer-reactive
description:
Führt die Unterscheidung zwischen proaktiven (Codegen/Builder) und reaktiven (Lint/Test) Enforcern als harness-weites Vokabular ein. Grundlage für alle anderen Trinity-Beads.

## Rationale

Analyse in mira-adapters zeigt: beide Enforcer-Typen haben verschiedene Stärken. Codegen macht falschen Code unmöglich, Lint fängt ihn ab. Ohne Vokabular-Trennung vermischen die Skills/Agents die beiden Konzepte und können keine sinnvollen Empfehlungen geben.

## Scope

- CLAUDE.md im Repo-Root: neuer Abschnitt 'Architecture Trinity Vocabulary'
- meta/AGENTS.md: Trinity-Begriffe dokumentieren
- README.md des neuen Plugins meta/plugins/architecture-trinity/: Vokabular als Einstieg
- Keine Code-Änderungen (reines Doku-Bead)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] CLAUDE.md enthält Abschnitt mit Definitionen: ADR, Helper, Enforcer-Proactive (Codegen/Builder), Enforcer-Reactive (Lint/Test)
- [ ] mira-adapters-Beispiele pro Kategorie zitiert (ADR-001, makeIdHelper, no-raw-id-concat)
- [ ] meta/plugins/architecture-trinity/README.md erstellt (Stub reicht, wird in CCP-6up ausgebaut)
- [ ] Keine Code-Änderungen, nur Docs
notes: Parent: CCP-6up. Blockiert: CCP-architecture-scout, CCP-enforcement-matrix, CCP-pflicht-sektion. Muss zuerst abgeschlossen sein, damit die anderen Beads konsistent vom Vokabular Gebrauch machen.
close_reason: Introduced four-term Architecture Trinity vocabulary (ADR, Helper, Enforcer-Proactive, Enforcer-Reactive) in CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and architecture-trinity/README.md stub with mira-adapters examples

title: [EPIC] Trinity-Harness: ADR/Helper/Enforcer als First-Class-Prozess-Primitiv
description:
Führt das ADR / Helper / Enforcer-Tripel (erkannt in mira-adapters) als systematisches Prozess-Primitiv im Beads-Harness ein. Ziel: Cross-Cutting-Contracts explizit und enforcement-fähig machen, bevor Bug-Cluster sie aufdecken.

## Hintergrund

Analyse von mira-adapters hat gezeigt: jede nachhaltige Architekturverbesserung hat drei Artefakte (ADR + Helper + Enforcer). Fehlt eines, kommen Bug-Cluster nach. Die erfolgreichsten Refactors (makeIdHelper/ESLint, Schema Codegen, Reconciliation-Spec-Check) folgen exakt diesem Muster. Die Fehlschläge clustern um implizite Contracts.

## Erweiterung: Enforcer ist zweigeteilt

- **Proactive (Codegen/Builder)**: macht falschen Code unmöglich (z.B. Schema-Codegen, FHIR-Builder, makeIdHelper als Fabrik)
- **Reactive (Lint/Test)**: fängt falschen Code ab (z.B. no-raw-id-concat, reconciliation-spec-check.ts)

Der Harness bekommt beide Typen als First-Class-Labels.

## Erweiterung: Package-Coverage-Matrix

Contract × Package × (ADR / Helper / Proactive / Reactive) als Matrix statt flacher Liste. Macht Hoist-Schulden und Pre-Trinity-Packages sichtbar.

## Scope

Neues Plugin meta/plugins/architecture-trinity/ plus Amendments an beads-workflow (create, wave-orchestrator, review-agent), business/council, dev-tools/project-context.

## Erfolgsindikatoren

- Jeder Bead mit Label 'touches-contract' hat die Pflicht-Sektion 'Architecture Contracts Touched'
- Project-context erzeugt eine Enforcement Matrix pro Repo
- wave-orchestrator blockt bei unvollständigen Tripeln
- review-agent erstellt Follow-up-Beads bei Contract-Drift
- allowlist-burndown tracked alle Allowlist-Einträge als Schulden
design: Repo: claude-code-plugins (alle Artefakte dort). Neues Plugin: meta/plugins/architecture-trinity/ mit architecture-scout (Agent), /adr-gap, /allowlist-burndown, /enforcement-matrix skills. Amendments an bestehende Plugins: beads-workflow/skills/create (Pflicht-Sektion), beads-workflow/skills/wave-orchestrator (Coverage-Gate), beads-workflow/agents/review-agent (Contract-Drift-Phase), business/skills/council (architecture mode), dev-tools/skills/project-context (Enforcement-Matrix-Sektion). cognovis-library wird später via library.yaml auf das neue Plugin verweisen — nicht Teil dieses Epics.
notes: Analyse-Grundlage: mira-adapters Commit-Inventar 2026-03-26 bis 2026-04-17 (414 Beads, 227 fix / 75 feat / 38 refactor / 25 docs). Tripel-Beispiele in mira-adapters: ADR-001 + makeIdHelper() + ESLint no-raw-id-concat (vollständig); ADR-004 + reconciliation-spec-check.ts (teilweise). Fehlende Tripel: Identity (mira-adapters-14s2), Writeback Safety (mira-adapters-agw6).

title: [harness-parity] Spike: migrate ONE agent (researcher) to skill format, verify in both tools
description:
Sub-bead of CCP-3rh. Proof-of-concept that one agent round-trips: Claude Code agent -> SKILL.md -> copied to both .claude/skills/ and .agents/skills/ -> invoked successfully from cld AND from codex CLI.

Picks the 'researcher' agent as the test subject because:
- It already has interesting tool constraints (disallowedTools: Write, Edit, Agent)
- Uses mcpServers (searxng, executive-circle, heypresto, open-brain)
- Modest scope (web research, not complex multi-phase orchestration)

Exit criteria for the spike:
- SKILL.md exists with researcher's instructions
- Invokable via $researcher from Codex CLI
- Invokable via prompt/skill-trigger from Claude Code
- Produces equivalent output quality in both tools on a sample query
- Findings documented: what worked, what was lossy, what broke

Depends on migration spec from prior sub-bead.
acceptance_criteria: Single SKILL.md works in both tools on the same test query; spike report documents friction points and translation decisions for fleet migration
notes:
Prior-art reference for the spike (session 2026-04-16):

**disler/pi-vs-claude-code** (https://github.com/disler/pi-vs-claude-code, 763 stars) — explicit comparison of Pi and Claude Code with working examples. Read during the spike to understand Pi's SKILL.md consumption if we ever want Pi as a third installer target.
close_reason: Superseded by work in cognovis/library. The harness-parity work is pivoting from 'BMAD-pattern installer' to 'fork disler/the-library, add Codex/multi-harness support'. New repo cognovis/library will host this work with its own beads (prefix CL). See cognovis/library README for continuation. Session summary: discovered that (1) Codex has first-class subagents + skills using the same SKILL.md format as Claude Code per the open agent skills standard, (2) BMAD-METHOD v6 confirmed dumb-copy-as-installer viability but is deploy-all which doesn't fit our heterogeneous projects, (3) disler/the-library's catalog+pointer architecture is a better fit — per-repo on-demand install via /library use X, (4) agents layer (.md frontmatter vs .toml) is the hardest portability problem, (5) hooks are cross-cutting and need separate strategy, (6) IndyDevDan's pattern of one catalog + many content repos generalizes well.

title: [harness-parity] Research: verify skill format compatibility + document migration spec
description:
Sub-bead of CCP-3rh. Before writing any code, verify the research assumptions:

1. **'Open agent skills standard' spec** — OpenAI Codex docs reference it. Find the canonical source (RFC? website? GitHub repo?). Confirm the field set Claude Code and Codex both support.
2. **Claude Code sidecar convention** — Codex supports agents/openai.yaml next to SKILL.md for per-platform metadata (icon, policy.allow_implicit_invocation, dependencies.tools). Does Claude Code have an equivalent? Plugin manifest only? Or nothing?
3. **Frontmatter extension fields** — document which fields are tool-specific vs universal. Known examples: user-invocable (in openai-codex plugin), allow-tools (potential Claude Code extension). Source a complete list from each tool's docs.
4. **cld script semantics** — read the current cld wrapper (~/.local/bin/cld or equivalent); document what it does at launch. This shapes cdx design.
5. **How tool scoping works in skill format** — if Claude Code agent used 'tools: [Read, Grep, Glob]', how is that expressed in skill format? Is it lost? Pushed into skill body as instructions? Declared in a sidecar?

Deliverable: docs/architecture/<date>-harness-parity/01-migration-spec.md with field mapping table, sidecar schema, field-loss audit, recommended canonical format.

9. **Hook logic vs. hook wiring.** Recommend one of: (a) per-tool separate hooks, (b) shared hook bodies + per-tool adapter configs, (c) push intent into skills. Give decision criteria.

title: [EPIC] Harness parity: shared agents/skills usable in Claude Code AND Codex (BMAD-pattern)
description:
## Goal

Make our agent/skill fleet usable from both Claude Code (via cld) and OpenAI Codex CLI (via new cdx) without dual-maintenance. Not about feature parity between the tools — about parity of OUR abstractions on top of them.

## Why now

Codex shipped first-class subagents + skills that use the same SKILL.md format as Claude Code (open agent skills standard). Both tools consume SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter (name, description). Install paths differ (.claude/skills/ vs .agents/skills/) but the file format is identical. BMAD v6 (bmad-code-org/BMAD-METHOD, v6.3.0 April 2026) confirmed this architecture works: their 'builder' is a dumb copy, not a transpiler.

## Prior art studied

- BMAD-METHOD v6: skills-only architecture, platform-codes.yaml drives install targets, no transpilation
- OpenAI Codex plugin for Claude Code (codex-plugin-cc, openai-codex plugin): already ships SKILL.md files that both tools load unmodified
- Claude Code built-in claude-code-guide agent: pattern for docs-query agents (we replicated as codex-guide)

## Scope

- ~35 Claude Code agents (.md files with tools:/model:/mcpServers: frontmatter) migrate to skills/<name>/SKILL.md where possible
- Agents that genuinely need Claude Code tools: restriction stay as agents; Codex equivalent (if needed) is a TOML custom agent
- Skills directory becomes the canonical source; installer copies to target tool's location
- cdx wrapper script parallels cld (bead-orchestrator invocation, review loop, etc.)

## Out of scope (for this epic)

- Feature parity between Claude Code and Codex (they're different tools, never going to match)
- Porting every Claude-Code-specific plugin (hooks, permissions, slash commands) to Codex
- Codex MCP server for our docs
acceptance_criteria: Any of our core agents (bead-orchestrator, review-agent, researcher) can be invoked from Codex CLI via cdx with equivalent behavior to cld; new SKILL.md files live in canonical location; installer copies to both targets; docs explain the migration path for remaining agents
notes: Scope extension (session 2026-04-16): hooks added as first-class concern alongside agents/skills. Hooks are the hardest portability problem because they're executable code tied to tool-specific event lifecycles — unlike skills (declarative content) and agents (session config). Strategy TBD by CCP-rma research; implementation in CCP-q7r.

New repo cognovis/library will host this work with its own beads (prefix CL). See cognovis/library README for continuation. Session summary: discovered that (1) Codex has first-class subagents + skills using the same SKILL.md format as Claude Code per the open agent skills standard, (2) BMAD-METHOD v6 confirmed dumb-copy-as-installer viability but is deploy-all which doesn't fit our heterogeneous projects, (3) disler/the-library's catalog+pointer architecture is a better fit — per-repo on-demand install via /library use X, (4) agents layer (.md frontmatter vs .toml) is the hardest portability problem, (5) hooks are cross-cutting and need separate strategy, (6) IndyDevDan's pattern of one catalog + many content repos generalizes well.

title: project-context.md Generator — aus Codebase initial erzeugen
description:
Einmal-Generator, der ein project-context.md (Constitution-Pattern aus Spec-Kit/BMAD) aus einer existierenden Codebase erzeugt. Inhalt: Tech-Stack, Architektur-Prinzipien, Module-Map (High-Level), etablierte Patterns, kritische Invarianten.

Input: Codebase-Root, CLAUDE.md, bestehende Architecture-Docs (falls vorhanden).
Output: docs/project-context.md — lesbar, editierbar, git-versioniert.

Wird von bead-orchestrator Phase 2 (CCP-1ul) konsumiert. Von session-close (CCP-ttt via CCP-xxx) aktualisiert falls neue Architektur-Entscheidungen getroffen werden.

Als Skill implementieren (/project-context), nicht als Agent. Triggerbar pro Projekt. Regeneration bewusst manuell, nicht automatisch (sonst Drift-Gefahr).

## Scenario
**Precondition:** Ein Claude-Code-Projekt mit Codebase-Root, optional vorhandenem `CLAUDE.md` und optional bestehenden Architektur-Dokumenten unter `docs/`. Kein `docs/project-context.md` existiert (oder existiert und soll bewusst überschrieben werden).
**Action:** Der User ruft das Skill `/project-context` im Projekt-Root auf.
**Expected Result:** Das Skill analysiert Codebase, `CLAUDE.md` und existierende Architektur-Docs und erzeugt `docs/project-context.md` mit den Sektionen Tech-Stack, Architektur-Prinzipien, Module-Map (High-Level), etablierte Patterns und kritische Invarianten. Die Datei ist menschenlesbar, editierbar und wird beim nächsten Commit versioniert. Das Skill schreibt nicht ohne Vorwarnung über eine existierende Datei.

### Test Data
- Repo 1: `~/code/claude-code-plugins` (dieses Repo — hat `CLAUDE.md`, Plugin-Struktur, keine bestehenden Architektur-Docs außer `docs/architecture/`).
- Repo 2: Mira-Repo (TypeScript/Aidbox, hat `CLAUDE.md` und mehrere Architektur-Dokumente, größere Module-Map).

### Variants
- Kein `CLAUDE.md` vorhanden — Skill fällt auf Codebase-Analyse zurück und markiert fehlende Quelle im Output.
- Keine existierenden Architektur-Docs — Module-Map wird rein aus Verzeichnisstruktur abgeleitet.
- Großes Monorepo (mehrere Sub-Packages) — Module-Map bleibt High-Level, keine Datei-für-Datei-Auflistung.
- `docs/project-context.md` existiert bereits — Skill fragt nach Überschreiben oder erzeugt Diff-Vorschlag.
- Gemischte Sprachen (Python + TypeScript + Shell) — Tech-Stack listet alle relevanten Runtimes und Paketmanager.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Skill /project-context generiert docs/project-context.md aus Codebase. 2. Template deckt ab: Tech-Stack, Architektur-Prinzipien, Module-Map, Patterns, Invarianten. 3. Manuelle Nachbearbeitung bleibt erwuenscht (nicht auto-regenerierbar). 4. Test: Auf claude-code-plugins und auf Mira-Repo ausfuehren, Output ist sinnvoll.
notes:
Plan Review Gate: CLEAN (advisory) — Reviewer flagged: no concrete template defined, invariants extraction strategy unspecified, overwrite UX not detailed, plugin registration missing. All addressed in implementation briefing.
Review loop: 1 iteration, status: CLEAN, quality: A, TDD: A

Output accurate and well-structured. AK4 on Mira repo still pending (needs separate session).

title: [INVESTIGATE] Intermittent cmux send failures between impl and reviewer surfaces
description:
## Problem

During wave orchestration on 2026-04-11 across 9 waves (~20 beads), the automatic
\`cmux send\` between bead-orchestrator (impl surface) and cmux-reviewer (reviewer surface)
failed intermittently. Approximately 50% of surface-to-surface sends required manual
intervention by the wave orchestrator.

## Observed Behavior

### Successful sends:
- aezd reviewer (surface:105) → impl (surface:99): Fix prompt delivered successfully
- sgu3 reviewer (surface:107) → impl (surface:103): Fix prompt + re-review delivered
- sgu3 impl → reviewer: \`cmux send --surface surface:107 "re-review"\` succeeded
  (we can see "OK surface:107 workspace:1" in impl scrollback)

### Failed sends requiring manual intervention:
- 3gw7: reviewer (surface:93) said "run bd close + git worktree remove" but didn't
  send session close to impl (surface:86)
- wq19: reviewer (surface:94) finished review but didn't trigger session close on impl
  (surface:89)
- 14er: reviewer (surface:92) finished but session close was not auto-triggered
- aezd: reviewer (surface:105) injected fixes, sent "re-review" → BUT the impl surface
  (surface:99) didn't react until wave orchestrator manually sent it
- Multiple beads: bead-orchestrator spawned reviewer correctly via \`cld -br\` but
  reviewer didn't auto-start review (cmux-shim stop hook issue?)

## Investigation Areas

### 1. cmux send timing
The reviewer may try to \`cmux send\` while the target surface is:
- Still processing a previous tool call
- In the middle of context compaction
- Showing a prompt but not actually ready for input
Check: Does cmux send buffer or drop messages if the target isn't at a prompt?

### 2. cmux-shim.sh vs cmux binary path confusion
```
zprofile sets:  CMUX_BUNDLED_CLI_PATH=$HOME/.claude/scripts/cmux-shim.sh
runtime shows:  CMUX_BUNDLED_CLI_PATH=/Applications/cmux.app/.../bin/cmux
```
The cmux app may override the env var when launching panes. This affects the stop hook
(notification filtering), not cmux send — but it may affect session lifecycle events
that trigger the send.

### 3. send vs send-key sequencing
The agents use:
```bash
cmux send --surface SURFACE "message"
cmux send-key --surface SURFACE enter
```
If the send lands but the enter doesn't (or arrives before the send is rendered),
the message sits in the input buffer without being submitted. This would explain
"message visible but not executed" symptoms.

### 4. Error handling in agent code
In \`agents/cmux-reviewer.md\` and \`agents/bead-orchestrator.md\`, the \`cmux send\`
calls don't check return codes. If send fails, the agent doesn't retry or notify.

## Files to Investigate

- \`beads-workflow/agents/cmux-reviewer.md\` — Lines that use \`cmux send\` to impl surface
- \`beads-workflow/agents/bead-orchestrator.md\` — Lines that spawn reviewer and send to it
- \`scripts/cmux-shim.sh\` — Stop hook intercept logic
- \`beads-workflow/skills/wave-orchestrator/references/error-recovery.md\` — Manual recovery docs

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Root cause identified for intermittent send failures
- [ ] Fix implemented (retry logic, send verification, or timing guard)
- [ ] Test: dispatch 3 beads in parallel, all reviewer↔impl transitions auto-complete without manual intervention
close_reason: Root cause identified and fixed: replaced two-call send+send-key pattern with atomic send 'text\n' + 2s retry at all 3 cmux send sites in cmux-reviewer.md; eliminates race condition causing ~50% of reviewer-to-impl transitions to fail silently

title: /vision-review skill: cadence-triggered vision re-evaluation with health score + draft ADRs
description:
New skill for periodically re-evaluating vision.md against current reality. Trigger-based (not pure calendar) per Perri + Torres. Produces a vision health score; contested principles generate draft ADRs (NOT silent edits — vision mutations always go through ADR flow).

**Why this exists**: user observation that vision documents (e.g. mira-adapters) get revisited every 2-3 weeks as new input lands, but there's no structured ritual for it. PO research confirmed nobody ships scheduled vision review tooling — biggest taught-but-ignored gap. This skill closes that gap with the smallest viable cadence.

## Triggers

Configurable in .claude/project-config.yml:

- **Quarterly cron** (default: every 90 days; overridable per project)
- **Signal: ADR contradicts principle** — any new ADR draft that mutates a principle fires /vision-review
- **Signal: N beads closed** — default N=15; per Torres, tie cadence to delivery not calendar
- **On-demand** — `/vision-review` manual invocation

## Process

1. Load: vision.md (principles + boundary table) + project-context.md + recently closed beads + open beads + recent ADRs
2. **Per-principle Y/N + evidence** — for each principle in vision.md Value Principles section: ask 'Is this still true? Y/N + cite evidence.'
3. **Council critique on contested** — for each contested principle: spawn /council --mode=architecture (CCP-bw6) for adversarial multi-perspective critique
4. **Draft ADRs** — generate `docs/adr/<NNNN>-vision-mutation-<slug>.md` per contested principle with:
   - `type: vision-mutation`
   - `supersedes: vision.md#principle-N`
   - `evidence: [user input + council findings]`
   - Status: draft (user reviews + commits separately — never silent vision.md edit)
5. **Vision health score** — % principles confirmed unchanged. Report at end.
6. **Re-author trigger** — if health score < 80%: 'Vision needs full re-author session — invoke /vision-author --refresh?'

## Time budget

< 20 min for routine review of a 5-principle vision. Per Torres adaptation: weekly habits don't fit project vision (too short cycle), but quarterly + signal-triggered hits the sweet spot.

## Output

- `docs/vision-review-<date>.md` — review report with per-principle Y/N + evidence + health score
- `docs/adr/<NNNN>-vision-mutation-*.md` — draft ADRs for contested principles (do NOT auto-merge into vision.md)
- Optional: bd create for newly-surfaced beads (e.g. 'document principle alignment for X')

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Skill SKILL.md exists with proper frontmatter
- [ ] All 4 triggers (cron / ADR signal / beads-closed signal / on-demand) work
- [ ] Per-principle Y/N + evidence dialog implemented
- [ ] /council --mode=architecture integration for contested principles
- [ ] Draft ADRs generated (NOT silent vision.md edits)
- [ ] Vision health score computed and reported
- [ ] Health score < 80% triggers re-author suggestion
- [ ] Smoke test on mira-adapters vision.md (after it's converted to principle format per CCP-w56 reframe)
- [ ] Cron schedule configurable per .claude/project-config.yml

## Depends on

- tense-gate bead (validates principles before computing health score; health calculation rejects principles with future-tense violations)
acceptance_criteria: Cadence-triggered review with per-principle Y/N + evidence; council critique on contested; draft ADRs (no silent edits); vision health score; <20 min for 5 principles; smoke-tested
notes: Cadence model from Teresa Torres (Continuous Discovery) adapted to project-vision rhythm. ADR-as-only-mutation-path from mira-agent's 2026-04-19 reframe. /council integration leverages already-planned CCP-bw6 (architecture mode). Sibling skill: /vision-author.

title: /vision-author skill: 7-question guided dialogue producing structured vision.md
description:
New skill producing a project-level vision.md through a structured PO-grade dialogue. Replaces ad-hoc vision authoring with a template synthesized from Pichler Product Vision Board (top section), Perri's Vision→Challenge hierarchy, Moore's positioning sentence, and JTBD principle language.

**Why this exists**: vision.md becomes load-bearing in Trinity-Harness — read by architecture-scout (CCP-2hd) for boundary checks, parsed by review-agent (CCP-wxv) for principle-violation drift detection, surfaced by bead-orchestrator Phase 2 (CCP-vwg) as prescriptive context. It must be authored consistently across projects, in present-tense only, with locked sections.

**Complementary to**:
- /spec-developer (feature-level Q&A, 21+ questions, 500-700 LOC spec)
- /epic-init (initiative-level dialog → beads)
- /vision (creative brainstorm, NOT structured authoring)
- /project-context (descriptive, auto-generated)

This skill is **project-level, prescriptive, hand-authored with guidance**.

## 7-question dialogue

1. **Vision Statement** — What positive change does the product bring about, and for whom? (1 sentence, present-tense)
2. **Target Group** — Who is primary user vs customer (if separate)?
3. **Core Need (JTBD)** — 'When [situation], users need to [motivation] so they can [outcome].'
4. **Positioning (Moore)** — 'For [target], who [need], [product] is a [category] that [benefit]. Unlike [alt], we [differentiator].'
5. **Value Principles** (load-bearing) — Name 3-5 invariants that MUST hold right now. Each present-tense, each testable.
6. **Business Goal** — One business reason this product exists (qualitative, not metric).
7. **Explicit 'NOT in vision' list** — Name 2 future-roadmap items you might be tempted to put here. (Forces author to disambiguate vision vs roadmap; items become beads.)

## Enforcement during dialogue

- **Tense gate** (depends on tense-gate bead) runs on every saved section
- **STUB rejection**: if user enters 'TBD' / 'to be decided' / leaves blank → skill prompts: 'this is a roadmap item, not a principle — file as bead?' and routes to bd create
- **Section contract**: locked H2 headers, no extra sections allowed
- **Boundary table**: question 5 also produces a locked-format boundary table (columns: rule | scope | source-section) for architecture-scout to parse

## Output

- `docs/vision.md` with 7 sections + locked boundary table format
- `docs/adr/0000-vision-initial.md` — genesis ADR (the 'decided' event for vision creation)
- Optional: bd create for items named in question 7 (the 'NOT in vision' list become explicit roadmap beads)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Skill SKILL.md exists with proper frontmatter
- [ ] 7-question adaptive dialog implemented (skip irrelevant follow-ups based on answers)
- [ ] Tense gate runs on every saved section (rejects future tense)
- [ ] STUB/TBD/blank-section rejection with reroute to bead creation
- [ ] Output template (references/vision-template.md) defines locked sections + boundary table format
- [ ] Genesis ADR auto-created at docs/adr/0000-vision-initial.md
- [ ] Smoke test: run on a fresh project (or claude-code-plugins itself) producing valid vision.md
- [ ] Edge case: --refresh mode for re-authoring an existing vision.md (loads current, walks through Q&A with current answers as defaults)

## Depends on

- tense-gate bead (the enforcement mechanism — without it, 'present-tense only' is just convention)
acceptance_criteria: 7-question dialog produces valid present-tense vision.md with locked sections; tense gate enforces; STUB rejection works; genesis ADR auto-created; smoke-tested
notes: Synthesized from PO research 2026-04-19: Pichler Product Vision Board + Perri's strategy hierarchy + Moore positioning + JTBD + Torres discovery cadence. Strongest insight: 'tense gate' as mechanical enforcement of vision = present-tense-only. Sibling skill: /vision-review.

title: conformance-reviewer agent: Phase 0 gate reading Critical Invariants from project-context
description:
Add a new lightweight reviewer agent to beads-workflow that runs as Phase 0 of bead-orchestrator. It reads the Critical Invariants section from docs/project-context.md plus the bead description and returns CONFORM or VIOLATION. No-op (auto-pass) when no invariants are declared.

**New agent** (beads-workflow/agents/conformance-reviewer.md):
- Model: haiku (cheap, fast)
- Tools: Read, Grep, Glob (read-only)
- Inputs: bead ID (or bead title+description+notes), path to docs/project-context.md
- Process:
  1. Read project-context.md, extract 'Critical Invariants' section
  2. If empty/missing: return { status: CONFORM, reason: 'no invariants declared' }
  3. For each invariant: check whether the bead description plausibly violates it (semantic check, not regex)
  4. Also check: if Reading Corpus has phase=bead-creation entries, verify the bead description references the listed docs OR explains why they don't apply
- Output: structured JSON { status: CONFORM|VIOLATION, findings: [{ invariant, concern, severity }] }
- Severity: BLOCKING (orchestrator stops) vs ADVISORY (orchestrator continues with warning)

**Orchestrator integration** (bead-orchestrator.md):
- New Phase 0.5 (between size-check and claim): spawn conformance-reviewer
- If status=VIOLATION with BLOCKING findings: stop, post findings to bead notes, exit
- If ADVISORY: log warnings, continue
- If CONFORM: continue normally
- Add a flag to skip (e.g. CONFORMANCE_SKIP=1) for emergency unblocks

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] conformance-reviewer agent file exists with proper YAML frontmatter
- [ ] Returns CONFORM when no invariants declared (no-op behavior verified)
- [ ] Returns VIOLATION with structured findings when bead clearly violates a stated invariant
- [ ] bead-orchestrator integrates as Phase 0.5
- [ ] Skip flag documented and works
- [ ] Smoke test on a fake bead that violates an invariant in this repo's project-context.md

**Depends on:** Reading Corpus bead (needs the template to know where Critical Invariants live and corpus surfacing pattern).
acceptance_criteria: conformance-reviewer agent exists; bead-orchestrator Phase 0.5 calls it; no-op when invariants empty; blocks/warns appropriately when violations found; smoke-tested
notes:
## Refinements (mira-agent review, 2026-04-19)

**Two run modes**, configurable in .claude/project-config.yml:
- `advisor` (default): findings surfaced as DECIDE items to user; orchestrator continues
- `gate`: BLOCKING findings stop bead-orchestrator at Phase 0; plan does not proceed

Default advisor so the agent doesn't block forward motion. Projects opt into gate when their vision/invariants are stable.

**Output is machine-parseable** (JSON or strict markdown blocks). Format must be consumable directly by wave-orchestrator and bead-orchestrator without re-parsing prose. Suggested:

```json
{
  "status": "CONFORM" | "VIOLATION",
  "mode": "advisor" | "gate",
  "findings": [
    { "rule": "vision §4 layer:platform must not depend on layer:application", "concern": "...", "severity": "BLOCKING" | "ADVISORY", "source": "docs/vision.md#L142" }
  ]
}
```

**Locked boundary table format in vision.md template.** The conformance-reviewer parses vision.md's §4 (or equivalent) boundary table. To make parsing reliable, lock the table format in a vision.md template that ships with /project-context (or a sibling skill). Suggested columns: rule | scope | source-section. This prevents projects from authoring boundary rules in unparseable prose.

**Drift check is sibling, not part of k1u.** Originally suggested as 'fold into k1u'. After more thought: split it out as CCP-???-drift (filed separately) because it has a different cadence (post-regeneration, not per-bead) and a different consumer (human reviewer / scheduled patrol, not orchestrator gate). Sharing the rules engine is fine; the trigger is different.

**Acceptance criteria (added):**
- [ ] mode: advisor|gate in .claude/project-config.yml schema
- [ ] Output schema documented (JSON-shape)
- [ ] vision.md template includes locked boundary table format
- [ ] CONFORMANCE_SKIP=1 env var bypass works
- [ ] Smoke test: advisor mode passes through with warnings; gate mode blocks orchestrator on BLOCKING finding
close_reason: Superseded by Trinity-Harness CCP-2hd (architecture-scout) + CCP-qcz (wave-orchestrator gate). Refinements migrated: advisor (default) vs gate run modes + machine-parseable JSON output + locked boundary table format in vision.md template + CONFORMANCE_SKIP=1 bypass. The conformance-review function is absorbed by architecture-scout (which already runs pre-/plan). See CCP-2hd and CCP-qcz.

title: Layer tagging on beads: optional schema field + /create enforcement when layers declared
description:
Add an optional 'layer' field to bead metadata, with conditional enforcement: required when docs/project-context.md declares a layers list in its Module Map section, optional otherwise. Wire the /create skill to prompt for and validate it.

**Schema change** (beads):
- Add 'layer' to bead labels/metadata (use existing label mechanism: 'layer:platform', 'layer:application', etc.)
- No DB migration needed if using labels; document as convention

**Project-context template change** (output-template.md):
- Module Map section gains an optional 'Layers' subsection. Format:
  ```markdown
  ### Layers (for layer:* tagging)
  - platform
  - application
  - agent
  - ig
  - catalog
  - pvs-adapter
  ```
- When absent: layer tagging is optional fleet-wide
- When present: /create skill MUST validate layer label against this list

**/create skill change** (beads-workflow/skills/create/SKILL.md):
- Phase 0+: read docs/project-context.md if present, extract Layers list
- If Layers declared: prompt user 'Which layer? [list]' and reject bd create call without valid layer:* label
- If Layers absent: skip layer prompt
- Use bd create --label='layer:<name>' (or whatever the labels syntax is) to attach

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] /create reads project-context.md and detects Layers section
- [ ] When Layers declared, /create rejects creation without a valid layer:* label
- [ ] When Layers absent, /create works as before (no breaking change)
- [ ] bd list / bd show display the layer label
- [ ] Smoke test: in a project with no Layers, behavior is unchanged; in a project with Layers, attempting to create a bead without one fails

**Depends on:** Reading Corpus bead (template structure must land first to keep one PR per template change).
acceptance_criteria: layer:* label optional fleet-wide; required when project-context declares Layers; /create enforces; backwards compatible
notes:
## Refinements (mira-agent review, 2026-04-19)

**Two enforcement modes**, configurable in .claude/project-config.yml:
- `warn` (default): /create suggests a layer label; bead can be created without one
- `hard-fail`: /create rejects bead creation without a valid layer:* label

Default is warn so adoption isn't blocked. Projects opt into hard-fail when their layer model is mature.

**Tag inheritance from parent bead.** When a child bead is created (parent-child dependency), inherit the parent's layer label by default. User can override at /create time. Same pattern beads already uses for other labels — keep consistency.

**Layers are configurable per-project, not a fixed enum.** Already in original description; reinforce: zero hard-coded layer values in the harness. mira-adapters has 6-7; a CLI tool project has 0-2. The /create skill reads the layer list from .claude/project-config.yml (or from project-context.md Module Map → Layers subsection).

**Acceptance criteria (added):**
- [ ] enforcement_mode: warn|hard-fail in .claude/project-config.yml schema
- [ ] /create defaults to warn mode when config absent
- [ ] Child bead creation inherits parent layer label by default
- [ ] User can override inherited layer at /create time
- [ ] Zero hard-coded layer enum values anywhere in harness code
close_reason: Superseded by Trinity-Harness CCP-d1q (Bead-Label-Set). Refinements migrated: layer:* added to Trinity label set + warn/hard-fail enforcement modes (default warn) + per-project configurable layers (no hard-coded enum) + parent→child layer inheritance with override. See CCP-d1q for the migrated content.

title: Reading Corpus section in /project-context template + bead-orchestrator surfacing
description:
Add a 'Reading Corpus' section to dev-tools/skills/project-context/references/output-template.md that maps phases to mandatory-read documents. Update the /project-context skill to generate this section from CLAUDE.md, ADRs, and package CLAUDE.md files. Update beads-workflow/agents/bead-orchestrator.md Phase 2 to surface the corpus to the implementer.

**Template addition** (output-template.md):

```markdown
## Reading Corpus

> *Mandatory reads per workflow phase. Bead orchestrator surfaces these to subagents.*

| Phase | Trigger | Must-Read Docs |
|-------|---------|----------------|
| bead-creation | /create skill, bd create | docs/vision.md, docs/adr/*.md |
| implementation | bead-orchestrator Phase 2 | docs/architecture/*.md, package CLAUDE.md if present |
| review | review-agent | docs/standards/*.md, ADRs touched by diff |
```

**Skill change** (project-context/SKILL.md):
- Phase 1 (Gather Sources): also detect docs/adr/, docs/architecture/, docs/standards/, packages/*/CLAUDE.md
- Phase 2 (Analysis): generate Reading Corpus rows from detected sources
- Phase 3 (Generate Output): include Reading Corpus as new section between Module Map and Established Patterns

**Orchestrator change** (bead-orchestrator.md Phase 2):
- After reading docs/project-context.md, parse Reading Corpus table
- For phase=implementation: read each listed file and inject as context for the implementer subagent
- If a listed file is missing: warn but don't block

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] /project-context generates a Reading Corpus section
- [ ] Re-running /project-context --force on this repo (claude-code-plugins) produces a non-empty corpus
- [ ] bead-orchestrator Phase 2 reads the corpus and injects implementation-phase docs
- [ ] No-op behavior preserved when no docs/ subdirs exist
- [ ] Tests in skills/project-context/tests/ updated
acceptance_criteria: Reading Corpus section generated by /project-context; bead-orchestrator Phase 2 surfaces it; tests updated; works on this repo as smoke test
notes:
## Refinements (mira-agent review, 2026-04-19)

**Reading Corpus is per-bead-type × per-layer, not a flat list.** A feature bead in layer:platform needs different reads than a refactor bead in layer:pvs-adapter. Flat 'always read these' will get ignored.

**Routing source: .claude/project-config.yml** (per project), not template-default. Projects without this file get a degenerate single-layer config. Example shape:

```yaml
reading_corpus:
  bead_creation:
    default: [docs/vision.md, docs/adr/]
    by_layer:
      platform: [docs/vision.md#layer-platform, docs/adr/ADR-005-identity.md]
      ig: [docs/vision.md#13, https://npm.cognovis.de]
  implementation:
    default: [docs/project-context.md]
    by_layer:
      pvs-adapter: [packages/pvs-adapter/CLAUDE.md, docs/architecture/pvs-integration.md]
```

**Named contract in project-context template.** The Reading Corpus section in output-template.md must be a named, parseable section (locked H2 header, table format) so other tools can consume it without re-parsing prose.

**Surface vision.md alongside project-context.md.** bead-orchestrator Phase 2 currently only loads project-context.md. Widen to also load vision.md if present. One-line change; massive payoff for prescriptive-rule injection.

**Bead-creation phase routing**: when /create runs, it should read vision.md primarily (boundaries) and ask the user for layer BEFORE finalizing. Bead orchestration phase reads project-context.md primarily (stack, patterns).

**Acceptance criteria (added):**
- [ ] .claude/project-config.yml schema documented
- [ ] Routing supports bead-type × layer matrix
- [ ] vision.md surfaced by orchestrator if present (no-op if absent)
- [ ] Reading Corpus section is named, parseable, lockable
close_reason: Superseded by Trinity-Harness equivalents. Refinements migrated: per-bead-type×layer routing + .claude/project-config.yml + named/parseable section + vision.md surfacing → CCP-0hr. vision.md as peer prescriptive doc → CCP-vwg. See those beads for the migrated content.

title: [EPIC] Constitution Pattern v2: project-context corpus, layer tagging, conformance review
description:
Extend the existing /project-context skill (Constitution Pattern) with three fleet-wide additions discovered during mira/mira-adapters planning:

1. **Reading Corpus per phase** — docs/project-context.md gains a 'phase → mandatory reads' manifest, surfaced by bead-orchestrator Phase 2 to the implementer
2. **Layer tagging** — beads schema gets an optional layer:* label, enforced when project-context.md declares a layers list in its Module Map
3. **Phase 0 Conformance Reviewer** — new beads-workflow agent that reads Critical Invariants from project-context.md and gates bead implementation; no-op when no invariants declared

**Why now:** mira-adapters has a 6-layer FHIR architecture (platform/application/agent/ig/catalog/pvs-adapter) and vision.md boundary rules that are routinely ignored when authors create beads without reading the docs. BMAD-METHOD research confirmed that a project-context.md manifest pattern is the right shape — we already ship /project-context, we just need to extend it. The mechanism is universal; the values are project-specific.

**Constraint:** Do NOT build mira-specific harness. Every change here is fleet-wide; mira-adapters becomes a consumer that fills out its own project-context.md.

**Out of scope:** Migrating existing projects (just regenerate /project-context --force per project as needed).
notes:
Source: research session 2026-04-19, BMAD method comparison. See open-brain timeline + the conversation that produced the 'three gaps' framing for mira-adapters.
## v1 vs v2 framing (added 2026-04-19)

**v1** (current): /project-context generates a descriptive 'Constitution document' once. bead-orchestrator Phase 2 consumes it for context injection.

**v2** (this epic): same skill, but the Constitution becomes two-sided and the harness becomes drift-aware:
- **Descriptive half** = project-context.md (auto-generated 'what IS': stack, modules, patterns, detected invariants)
- **Prescriptive half** = vision.md (hand-written 'what SHOULD be': layer boundaries, governance, contracts) — recognized as a first-class harness file alongside project-context.md
- **Enforcement** = layer tagging on beads + Phase 0 conformance review
- **Drift detection** = compare descriptive vs prescriptive, surface mismatches as DECIDE items

## Routing principle (clarified)

- vision.md is primary input during **bead creation** (boundaries, governance — what's ALLOWED)
- project-context.md is primary input during **orchestration / implementation** (stack, patterns — what's THERE)
- Both feed the Reading Corpus, but with phase-specific routing

## Per-project config

A new file .claude/project-config.yml (or equivalent) carries per-project values: layers list, enforcement mode (warn vs hard-fail), routing rules per bead-type × layer. Projects without this file get degenerate defaults (single layer, warn mode, flat corpus) — no harness surprise.

## Source of refinements

mira/mira-adapters agent review of CCP-w56/u87/660/k1u, 2026-04-19. Confirms harness shape, lifts proposal from project-local (mira's edti.10/.11/.12) to fleet-wide. Flags routing-by-bead-type, configurable enforcement modes, and machine-parseable conformance output as essential.
close_reason: Superseded by Trinity-Harness epic CCP-6up. Constitution Pattern v2 and Trinity-Harness were solving the same meta-problem from different angles (doc-first vs contract-first). Trinity is more concrete and P1; v2 refinements migrated to Trinity children (CCP-0hr, CCP-vwg, CCP-d1q, CCP-2hd, CCP-qcz, CCP-wxv). Vision-skill children (CCP-2q2 tense-gate, CCP-hf1 /vision-author, CCP-a67 /vision-review) reparented to CCP-6up.

title: [FEATURE] wave-orchestrator: Coverage-Completeness-Gate vor Wave-Start
description:
Erweitert beads-workflow/skills/wave-orchestrator um ein Gate, das vor Wave-Start prüft: jeder berührte Contract muss ADR+Helper+Enforcer haben ODER einen offenen Tripel-Bead mit P1/P0.

## Gate-Logik

1. Aggregiere architecture-scout-Reports aller Beads in der Wave
2. Identifiziere berührte Contracts (aus 'Architecture Contracts Touched'-Sektion der Beads)
3. Pro Contract: prüfe Enforcement-Matrix-Status
4. Falls GAP: Wave pausiert mit Meldung 'Missing trinity for contract X in package Y'
5. Empfehlung: erzeuge Tripel-Beads zuerst, setze Dep, dann Wave retry

## Post-Wave-Audit

Nach Wave-Ende:
- Spawne 'Triple Audit'-Subagent
- Prüfe: wurden neue Helper ohne ADR+Enforcer geschaffen?
- Prüfe: wurden dieselben Bugs mehrfach gefixt (→ fehlender Enforcer)?
- Auto-Follow-up-Beads
design: Gate ist additiv zum bestehenden wave-flow. Nutzt den architecture-scout-Agent direkt via Agent-Tool. Coverage-Matrix wird vor Wave-Start frisch generiert (nicht gecacht).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] beads-workflow/skills/wave-orchestrator um pre-wave-gate erweitert
- [ ] Gate nutzt architecture-scout Output + Enforcement-Matrix
- [ ] Konfigurierbar: strict (block) vs. advisory (warn) per Repo-Setting
- [ ] Post-Wave Triple-Audit implementiert
- [ ] Doku: references/wave-coverage-gate.md
- [ ] Integration-Test mit 2-Bead-Wave (eine mit vollständigem Tripel, eine mit Gap)
notes:
Parent: CCP-6up. Abhängig von: architecture-scout (CCP-2hd) + Enforcement-Matrix (CCP-vwg). P2 — aktiviert volles Gating.
## Refinement migrated from CCP-k1u (conformance-reviewer, superseded 2026-04-19)

The wave-orchestrator Coverage-Gate should also support **two modes** (per .claude/project-config.yml):

- `advisor` (default): coverage gaps surfaced as warnings; wave proceeds
- `gate`: incomplete Trinity tripel BLOCKS wave start

Default advisor so adoption isn't blocked. Projects opt into gate when contract maturity is high. Mirrors the architecture-scout (CCP-2hd) mode pattern for consistency.

title: [FEATURE] Hoist-Trigger-Mechanismus: ADR-Frontmatter + /adr-gap skill
description:
ADRs bekommen Frontmatter-Feld 'hoist_when', das eine Bedingung ausdrückt (z.B. 'second adapter exists'). Ein periodischer Check prüft die Bedingung und erstellt automatisch den Hoist-Bead, wenn sie eintritt.

## Problem

ADR-001 in mira-adapters sagt: 'hoist makeIdHelper to adapter-common when adapter #2 exists'. Adapter #2 (pvs-charly) existiert inzwischen — aber niemand hat den Hoist gemacht. Stummer Tech-Debt.

## Lösung

ADR-Frontmatter:
```yaml
---
status: accepted
contract: id-taxonomy
applies_to: [pvs-x-isynet]
hoist_when:
  condition: second_package_implements_contract
  target: adapter-common
  trigger_bead_title: '[HOIST] makeIdHelper to adapter-common'
---
```

## Check

scripts/adr-hoist-check.sh läuft periodisch (CI, oder manuell via /adr-gap):
- Parst alle ADRs mit hoist_when-Frontmatter
- Evaluiert Bedingung (applied-to vs. aktuelle Package-Nutzung)
- Falls Bedingung wahr + Bead existiert nicht: bd create [HOIST]

## Zusätzlich: /adr-gap skill

On-Demand-Audit: 'welche ADRs haben Hoist-Schulden? welche Packages sind pre-trinity?'
design: Check als Python-Skript (PyYAML für Frontmatter-Parsing — PEP723 mit uv). Evaluator für Conditions ist stecker-basiert: vordefinierte Conditions (second_package_implements_contract, allowlist_entry_exists_for, etc.) statt arbiträrer Expressions.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] ADR-Frontmatter-Spec dokumentiert in meta/plugins/architecture-trinity/references/adr-frontmatter.md
- [ ] scripts/adr-hoist-check.sh (oder .py) erkennt fällige Hoists
- [ ] Skill /adr-gap erstellt (meta/plugins/architecture-trinity/skills/adr-gap/)
- [ ] Skill zeigt Hoist-Schulden + pre-trinity-packages
- [ ] Optional: Auto-Bead-Create mit --create-beads Flag
- [ ] Test-Fixture: ADR mit hoist_when + Repo-State, der Bedingung erfüllt
notes: Parent: CCP-6up. Unabhängig von anderen Beads — parallel machbar. P2.

title: [FEATURE] Skill /allowlist-burndown: Allowlists als zeitliche Schulden tracken
description:
Neuer Skill, der alle Allowlist-Einträge im Repo scannt (ESLint-disables, Reconciliation-Allowlists, 'as any'-Kommentare) und als überfällige Schulden mit Zielbead listet.

## Rationale

In mira-adapters sind Allowlist-Einträge wie mapAllLdtToFhir, Krabllink-Mapper etc. verzögerte Verpflichtungen. Sie gehören in den Workflow als eigener Objekt-Typ, nicht als Kommentar im Code.

## Scan-Quellen (generisch, Projekt-Konfiguration)

- ESLint: eslint-disable-next-line, eslint-disable + datei-lokal + rule-opt-outs
- TS: '// @ts-expect-error', '// @ts-ignore', 'as any'-Casts (wenn Policy das verbietet)
- Projekt-spezifische Allowlists: Pattern im config file definieren (scripts/*-check.ts)
- TODO/FIXME/HACK-Kommentare mit '[allowlist]'-Tag

## Output

Tabelle pro Allowlist-Eintrag:
- Datei:Zeile
- Typ (eslint-disable, ts-ignore, config-allowlist, tagged-comment)
- Kontext (Zeile drumherum, 3 Zeilen)
- Existiert Bead? (Suche nach 'allowlist-debt' + Datei-Referenz)
- Empfehlung: Bead anlegen mit [BURNDOWN]-Präfix
design: Skill läuft on-demand. Scan-Logik als Python-Skript (stdlib reicht für Regex-Scans). Bead-Erstellung nur bei explizitem --create-beads Flag — Standard ist Report-Only.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Skill meta/plugins/architecture-trinity/skills/allowlist-burndown/SKILL.md existiert
- [ ] scripts/allowlist-scan.py (uv/PEP723 oder stdlib) scannt 4 Quellen
- [ ] Ausgabe als Markdown-Report mit Tabelle
- [ ] Konfigurierbare Patterns via .trinity.yml im Repo-Root
- [ ] Optional: --create-beads Flag erzeugt [BURNDOWN]-Beads mit Label 'allowlist-debt'
- [ ] Unit-Test mit Fixture-Repo (3 Beispiel-Allowlists)
notes: Parent: CCP-6up. Abhängig von: Vokabular-Split + Enforcement-Matrix (damit der Report in die Matrix referenziert werden kann). P2.

title: [FEATURE] review-agent: Contract-Drift-Phase mit Auto-Follow-up-Beads
description:
Erweitert beads-workflow/agents/review-agent um eine Phase 'Contract Drift' NACH dem semantischen Review. Erkennt neue implizite Contracts und erzeugt Follow-up-Beads mit [ADR-NEEDED], [HELPER-NEEDED], [ENFORCER-PROACTIVE-NEEDED], [ENFORCER-REACTIVE-NEEDED]-Tags.

## Problem

review-agent prüft heute semantische Korrektheit + Acceptance Criteria. Er erkennt aber nicht 'hier wurde ein impliziter Contract eingeführt, der nur in einer Datei existiert'.

## Drei Fragen pro Diff

1. **Neue stringly-typed Patterns?** (Regex auf Literale in kritischen Pfaden, neue magische Strings in IDs/Routes/Scopes)
2. **Neue Invarianten nur in Tests ausgedrückt?** (Test-only Contracts = späterer Drift)
3. **Neue Duplikation gegenüber bestehendem Helper?** (umgangene makeIdHelper, inline-SQL statt TableHandler)

Plus: neue 'as any' (TS-spezifisch), neue raw-FHIR-Konstruktion (mira-spezifisch, aber generalisierbar als 'Builder bypass'), neue handwritten Row-Types.

## Output

Advisory Findings (nicht-blockierend für Merge) mit automatischer Bead-Erstellung:
- [ADR-NEEDED] <titel> — Contract erkannt, nicht dokumentiert
- [HELPER-NEEDED] <titel> — Pattern dreimal wiederholt
- [ENFORCER-PROACTIVE-NEEDED] — Codegen/Builder wäre sinnvoll
- [ENFORCER-REACTIVE-NEEDED] — Lint/Test wäre sinnvoll

Beads werden mit Dependency auf den geschlossenen Bead erstellt.
design: Phase läuft NACH semantic review + AC-check. Implementiert als zusätzlicher Prompt-Abschnitt im review-agent. Bead-Erstellung via Bash-Tool (bd create) aus dem Agent heraus.

title: quick-fix agent: breaks autonomous chain at session-close handoff (no auto-trigger)
description:
## Problem

Wenn der bead-orchestrator auf quick-fix umroutet (effort=small+task), beendet der quick-fix-Agent die Implementierung erfolgreich, schreibt Commits + Changelog + syncs, und zeigt dann einen Recap wie:

> "Bead status: in_progress — run session-close to merge and close."

Das ist ein Handoff an den **User** — der quick-fix-Agent triggert session-close NICHT selbst. Folge: Die Shell sitzt idle am Prompt (`❯`), main bleibt beim Dispatch-HEAD, und der Bead bleibt `in_progress`.

Das bricht die Auto-Orchestration, besonders für den wave-orchestrator, der nach der Regel "NEVER trigger session close" nicht selber einspringen darf.

## Reproduktion

Real passiert bei mira-adapters-ixgr (2026-04-17, workspace:22 surface:141):
1. `./wave-dispatch.sh mira-adapters-ixgr` → full-mode-Start
2. bead-orchestrator Phase 0 ruft quick-fix auf (effort auto-estimated = small)
3. quick-fix implementiert (25min), 22 Tests gruen
4. quick-fix invokes `cld --agent core:session-close` subagent
5. Session-close subagent macht changelog + issues sync → **committed auf worktree-branch**, aber KEIN merge zu main und KEIN bd close
6. quick-fix-Agent liefert Recap "Next: run session-close to merge and close"
7. Shell idle, Bead in_progress, Wave-Orchestrator stuck

Detail: Das Worktree hat 5 Commits (impl + tests + changelog + issues + state), ist aber nicht gemergt. User musste manuell "session close" in die Shell tippen.

## Mögliche Root Causes (zu verifizieren)

1. Der innere `cld --agent core:session-close` Subagent-Call macht nur Phase 1-3 (commit/changelog/sync) aber nicht Phase 4 (merge+push+close-bead). Seine Instruction ist evtl. unvollständig.
2. Quick-fix-Agent hat einen falschen Exit-Pfad: er sagt "triggere session-close" statt ihn tatsächlich zu triggern.
3. `cld --agent core:session-close` bricht beim Merge-Step selbst ab (evtl. wegen Dolt-Bug oder anderem Fehler) und terminiert silent.

## Erwartung

Der quick-fix-Agent muss die Kette genauso schliessen wie der full bead-orchestrator: Nach erfolgreicher Implementation+Review muss session-close **vollständig** laufen (merge feature→main, push, bd close) — autonom, ohne User-Handoff.

## Akzeptanzkriterien

- [ ] Quick-fix-Agent instrumentieren um zu zeigen was passiert: wird session-close tatsaechlich aufgerufen? Wo terminiert es?
- [ ] Root Cause verifiziert (Subagent-Instruction unvollständig / Exit-Path falsch / session-close silent failure)
- [ ] Fix: quick-fix-Agent fuehrt session-close autonom und vollstaendig bis bd closed + main advanced aus
- [ ] Regressionstest: quick-fix-Bead von Start bis bd closed ohne User-Input
- [ ] Wave-Orchestrator kann wieder darauf vertrauen, dass quick-fix-Beads autonom schliessen

## Bezug

- Real-Fall: mira-adapters/mira-adapters-ixgr (2026-04-17)
- Wave-Orchestrator-Regel "NEVER trigger session close" steht im Widerspruch zu diesem Failure-Mode. Entweder muss quick-fix autonom schliessen, oder der wave-orchestrator braucht einen Escape-Hatch fuer genau diesen Fall.

## Optionale Diskussion

Sollte der wave-orchestrator einen `--auto-close-on-handoff` Flag bekommen, der nach X Min session_close erkannten Idle-State manuell triggert? (Workaround, nicht Fix)

title: session-close + worktree-manager: Turn-Log konsumieren
description:
Integration der Worktree-Turn-Log-Pipeline in core:session-close und core:worktree-manager. Konsumiert die von open-brain-9tt erzeugte .worktree-turns.jsonl via open-brain-x7s Endpoint.

## Depends on

- open-brain-x7s (API endpoint /api/worktree-session-summary)
- open-brain-9tt (Turn-Log Hook + JSONL-Staging)

## core:session-close

Neuer Step in der Orchestrierung (vor oder in Step 13 "Learnings + Session Summary"):

1. Prüfe ob .worktree-turns.jsonl im aktuellen Arbeits-Root existiert.
   - Nicht vorhanden -> No-op, KEIN Error, KEIN Warning (Main-Repo-Sessions sind Normalfall).
   - Vorhanden aber leer -> Datei loeschen, weiter.
2. Lies alle Zeilen, parse als turns[].
3. POST /api/worktree-session-summary mit {worktree, bead_id (falls ableitbar), project, turns}.
4. Bei 202 Accepted: Datei loeschen.
5. Bei Fehler: Datei behalten, Warnung im Session-Close-Report (aber Session-Close nicht abbrechen).

Ersetzt langfristig den Observation-basierten Teil von summarize.py. Dessen Deaktivierung ist NICHT Teil dieses Beads — separate Entscheidung.

## core:worktree-manager

Pre-Remove Check beim Worktree-Loeschen:
- Wenn .worktree-turns.jsonl existiert und > 0 Bytes -> interaktive Rueckfrage: "Worktree hat ungespeichertes Turn-Log (N Eintraege). Vermutlich ist kein session-close gelaufen. Trotzdem loeschen? [y/N]"
- Bei y: loeschen wie bisher.
- Bei n: abbrechen, User-Hinweis "Fuehre erst session-close aus."

## Vokabular

Der Name "session-close" bleibt aus Kompatibilitaetsgruenden, obwohl in diesem Kontext "worktree-close" treffender waere. Nicht in diesem Bead umbenennen.

- [ ] core:session-close Agent-Definition liest .worktree-turns.jsonl vor Session-Summary-Step
- [ ] POST an /api/worktree-session-summary mit korrektem Payload-Schema
- [ ] Erfolgreicher POST -> .worktree-turns.jsonl loeschen
- [ ] Fehlerhafter POST -> Datei behalten, Warnung im Report, Session-Close faehrt fort
- [ ] .worktree-turns.jsonl nicht vorhanden -> stiller Skip, KEIN Error/Warning
- [ ] core:worktree-manager Pre-Remove: prueft .worktree-turns.jsonl Groesse
- [ ] Wenn Turn-Log non-empty -> interaktive Warnung + Confirm vor Loeschen
- [ ] Dokumentation in agents/core/session-close.md erwaehnt neuen Step
- [ ] Dokumentation in agents/core/worktree-manager.md erwaehnt Pre-Remove-Check
- [ ] Manuelle Integration-Verifikation: Fake-Worktree mit JSONL, session-close laeuft durch, Datei weg, Summary in open-brain

description:

Mode 1 — bulk deploy (BMAD pattern): 'scripts/install-skills --all' copies everything to target tool dir. Good for fresh machine setup, CI, team onboarding.

Both modes share the same canonical source and manifest (library.yaml-style pointer catalog). The Library's schema is worth adopting — cleaner than BMAD's CSV.

title: wave-status: idle heuristic uses only last 5 lines, misreads active agents as idle
description:

When a claude-code agent is actively thinking (e.g. 'Newspapering... 12m 2s'), the prompt character '❯' is still visible on the bottom line — this trips the idle regex even though the agent is mid-run. - Check for 'thinking' markers (Newspapering/Baking/Crunched/Churned) before declaring idle
